next_step_text: Confidence 30% 8th of July - RFQ/RFI submission ,evaluation will take 3 weeks 25th June - Subsequent to AlHakim's communications Omar called Dan to say we should expect technical clarifications next week and some draft usage numbers. 18th of June 2026 - intro request shared with the prospect to schedule for a meeting Next week to evaluate our position . 15th June - Spoke to Omar, the war has cause

our_open_deliverables.text: Send meeting slide deck to Omar Kaaki
Share available demo time slots for AI functionality demo
Provide template for capturing user counts and module selections after demo

buyer_dependent_items.text: Provide usage numbers across all business units for budgetary estimate

competitive_position.summary: SAP Ariba is the incumbent being displaced, not a live competitive threat; buyer is unhappy with Ariba UX, rising costs, and post-SAP stagnation. October 2026 RFP launch will introduce new competitors (Coupa, GEP, Ivalua, Jaggaer, potential SAP retention offer). Zycus holds early influence via BRD response and demo but no EB lock or exclusive track. Current strongest threat is the open RFP process itself and the risk of a competitor with C-level relationships or aggressive pricing winning executive sponsorship before Zycus maps the CFO/board.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: Ariba described as unintuitive and cumbersome, requiring extensive training and causing low user adoption. Post-acquisition by SAP, Ariba experienced reduced innovation and increasing licensing fees without enhanced functionality.
competitors.how_we_win: Ariba is being replaced due to poor UX, rising costs, and lack of innovation. Emphasize Zycus's AI depth (Merlin autonomous negotiation, AI intake, ANA), unified S2P platform with intuitive UX, proven multi-ERP SAP ECC integration, and phased low-risk implementation as the wedge vs Ariba complexity and stagnation.

champion_strength.source: SF OpportunityContactRole (Decision Maker), Next_Step__c timeline, Avoma calls 2025-10-14 and 2026-04-23, MEDDPICC CRM, deal pulse (last two-way touch 2026-04-28, rep outreach 2026-06-25)
champion_strength.summary: Champion strength is developing to strong on advocacy and internal influence but cooling on responsiveness. Omar Kaaki (Director, Group Procurement, Decision Maker) is actively driving the evaluation, multi-threading internally, and co-planning the 3-year transformation roadmap with Zycus. He requested proposal process on 2026-04-28 and introduced IT lead Nada Hallal. However, last two-way buyer touch was 64 days ago (2026-04-28 incoming email), and usage numbers promised by end June 2026 are now 1 day overdue. Rep-initiated outreach on 2026-06-25 (6 days ago) states Omar called to say clarifications and numbers expected this week, but no delivery yet. Risk: champion momentum weakening if usage numbers and budgetary estimate stall, reducing advocacy strength heading into October RFP when competitive field opens.

sources: MEDDPICC CRM: Omar is Decision Maker but needs board approval; budget owner is group upper management (likely CFO), not Omar
Avoma calls 2025-10-14 and 2026-04-23: no CFO or C-level executive present
SF OpportunityContactRole: Omar KAAKI = Decision Maker; Nada Hallal (IT Director), Naveen Agnel Pinto (Senior Manager Procurement, Influencer) — no EB/CFO role listed
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer (CFO or C-level budget owner who will approve the contract and sign) is not identified or engaged. Omar Kaaki (Director, Group Procurement, Decision Maker per SF contact role) is running the evaluation and stated on 2025-10-14 call that he 'will need board approval' and was offered co-presentation to CFO/board (MEDDPICC CRM). Omar has not named the EB, introduced Zycus to the CFO/board, or confirmed when EB engagement will occur. Budget owner is inferred to be 'group upper management (likely CFO/Exec), not Omar; exact owner unspecified' (MEDDPICC CRM). No EB or CFO appears on either buyer call (2025-10-14, 2026-04-23 Avoma manifest). Without EB identification and active sponsorship before October 2026 RFP launch, Zycus risks losing executive-level advocacy to a competitor with C-level relationships or stalling at commercial approval. Next move: map the CFO/board sponsor through Omar and secure executive connect to co-present the business case and budgetary proposal before RFP launch.

recommended_moves: Re-engage Omar to secure overdue usage numbers and co-author the budgetary proposal before month-end
Confirm SAP ECC connector availability and provide integration specifications to Nada and Omar
Map the CFO or C-level budget owner through Omar and propose executive connect to co-present the business case
